Following the completion of the third Four Quartets poem , The Dry Salvages , Eliot 's health declined and he stayed in Shamley Green , Surrey while he recovered.
During this time , Eliot started writing Little Gidding.

sentences.source_text: E. M. Forster did not like Eliot 's emphasis on pain and responded to the poem : " Of course there 's pain on and off through each individual 's life... You can 't shirk it and so on. But why should it be endorsed by the schoolmaster and sanctified by the priest until the fire and the rose are one when so much of it is caused by disease and bullies ? sentences.source_text: Yorick's journey starts in Calais, where he meets a monk who begs for donations to his convent.

He and the monk exchange their snuff-boxes.
He buys a chaise to continue his journey.

sentences.source_text: Without a passport at a time when England is at war with France (Sterne traveled to Paris in January 1762, before the Seven Years' War ended), he risks imprisonment in the Bastille.
Yorick decides to travel to Versailles where he visits the Count de B**** to acquire a passport.
When Yorick notices the count reads Hamlet, he points with his finger at Yorick's name, mentioning that he is Yorick.
The count mistakes him for the king's jester and quickly procures him a passport.
Yorick fails in his attempt to correct the count, and remains satisfied with receiving his passport so quickly.
Yorick returns to Calais, and continues his voyage to Italy after staying in Paris for a few more days.

triples.o: Paris for a few more days

sentences.source_text: Authorities say Charleston parked his vehicle nearby and then popped the hood as if there were something wrong.
Then he walked to the CVS, preparing to enter.
When agents confronted him, Charleston ran, got in his car and traveled about 75 yards as agents opened fire.

"There were several FBI vehicles that were rammed or were hit by the suspect's vehicle when he was trying to flee. One government vehicle sustained heavy damage to its front and side, and another government SUV ended up on its side.

sentences.source_text: That's how the two agents sustained their injuries," Emmett said.

sentences.source_text: After Morton Mintz broke the story in July 1962 , there was a substantial public outcry.
The Kefauver Harris Amendment was passed unanimously by Congress in October 1962 to strengthen drug regulation.
Companies were required to demonstrate the efficacy of new drugs , report adverse reactions to the FDA , and request consent from patients participating in clinical studies.

sentences.source_text: The drug testing reforms required " stricter limits on the testing and distribution of new drugs " to avoid similar problems.

sentences.source_text: The new laws were not without controversy.

sentences.source_text: (CNN) -- When I was 7 years old, my mom took me to the doctor because I had lost a lot of weight, despite a voracious appetite.
My family had just moved from the San Francisco Bay Area to Pasadena, California, and this visit to the doctor's office was my introduction to a man who would come to have a huge impact on my life.

triples.o: the San Francisco Bay Area to Pasadena, California

triples.s: this visit to the doctor's office
triples.o: my introduction to a man who would come to have a huge impact on my life

sentences.source_text: His name was Dr. Robert Deputy, and he was the pediatrician who diagnosed me with diabetes.

triples.o: the pediatrician who diagnosed me with diabetes

sentences.source_text: He spoke to me directly, rather than to my mother, and he asked me questions: Did I feel thirsty?
Did I need to urinate during the night?
The answer to both questions was yes.
He asked for a urine sample and the result was immediate.
Dr. Deputy told me and my mother that my body wasn't processing sugar properly.

triples.o: me and my mother that my body wasn't processing sugar properly

sentences.source_text: I had diabetes.
That is how my 40-year journey with diabetes began.
I was checked into a hospital to learn how to test my urine for sugar, give myself injections of insulin, follow a diabetic diet and control my diabetes.
I spent several days in the hospital learning how to manage my disease, and then they sent me home.
Dr. Deputy would call our house each afternoon to check on me and see how I was doing.
Much to my parents' chagrin -- and to my delight -- he wanted me to be self sufficient and responsible for monitoring myself.

triples.o: me to be self sufficient and responsible for monitoring myself

sentences.source_text: I refused to let my parents give me injections or test my urine.

triples.o: my parents give me injections or test my urine

sentences.source_text: This demand for self sufficiency turned out to be one of my best life lessons, and not just for managing my diabetes.

triples.o: one of my best life lessons

sentences.source_text: While some diabetics may feel their disease is a burden, I feel that being diagnosed with diabetes was a blessing in disguise.

triples.o: being diagnosed with diabetes was a blessing in disguise

sentences.source_text: It forced our family to eat healthily, and it taught me about good nutrition -- something I practice every day.
I know carbohydrate counts in most foods, and I understand dietary concepts that most people don't even care about.

triples.o: dietary concepts that most people don't even care about

sentences.source_text: Being active is also a way to burn off high blood sugar, so when my urine tested positive for glucose, my older sister Robin was in charge of exercising me.

triples.o: a way to burn off high blood sugar

sentences.source_text: We had countless jump-rope contests, bike rides and roller-skating excursions, keeping me and my three sisters active and fit.

triples.o: countless jump-rope contests, bike rides and roller-skating excursions

triples.s: countless jump-rope contests, bike rides and roller-skating excursions
triples.o: me and my three sisters active and fit

sentences.source_text: And I felt like a little mad scientist, using a test tube with drops of water, urine and fizzy tablets to see if there was sugar in my urine.

triples.o: a test tube with drops of water, urine and fizzy tablets
if there was sugar in my urine

sentences.source_text: Fortunately, over time, technology improved.
First, test strips were developed that could be dipped in urine to reveal whether sugar was present in it.

triples.o: that could be dipped in urine to reveal whether sugar was present in it
whether sugar was present in it

sentences.source_text: But the truth is, testing urine for sugar was a primitive method.
By the time sugar gets into the urine, it's a signal that the body doesn't have enough insulin to process sugar.

triples.o: a signal that the body doesn't have enough insulin to process sugar

sentences.source_text: And the urine test didn't give an actual number for the amount of sugar in the urine.

triples.o: an actual number for the amount of sugar in the urine

sentences.source_text: It simply gave a color reading that corresponded with a range of how much sugar was present in the urine.

triples.o: a color reading that corresponded with a range of how much sugar was present in the urine
a range of how much sugar was present in the urine

sentences.source_text: Inaccurate was really an understatement when you talk about urine testing.

triples.o: really an understatement when you talk about urine testing

sentences.source_text: Home blood glucose testing kits became available in the early 1980s, and they were a huge move toward precise control and management of diabetes.

triples.o: a huge move toward precise control and management of diabetes

sentences.source_text: With the kit, the diabetic uses a device to poke a finger and produce a drop of blood.

triples.o: a device to poke a finger and produce a drop of blood

sentences.source_text: The blood is placed on a test strip that fits into a device that produces a reading of the blood glucose within seconds.

triples.o: a reading of the blood glucose within seconds

sentences.source_text: While I initially balked at the idea of pricking my finger several times a day, now I can't imagine not doing it in order to monitor what my blood sugar is doing.

triples.o: the idea of pricking my finger several times a day
it in order to monitor what my blood sugar is doing

sentences.source_text: The 1980s also saw the advent of the insulin pump.

triples.o: the advent of the insulin pump

sentences.source_text: This device is the size of a pager and contains a reservoir filled with insulin.
The reservoir connects to a tube that infuses the insulin into the body with a tiny plastic shunt.

triples.o: the insulin into the body with a tiny plastic shunt

sentences.source_text: So rather than taking multiple injections to mimic the body's insulin patterns, the pump drips insulin into the body to keep blood sugars constant, then gives extra amounts with meals.
Another life-altering development in diabetes, which I have discovered in the last year, is the continuous glucose monitor, or CGM.

triples.o: the continuous glucose monitor in the last year

sentences.source_text: This is a small device inserted into the tissue -- usually the abdomen, hip or leg -- and it has a tiny sensor that reads the glucose level of the body's fluids.

triples.o: a small device inserted into the tissue
the glucose level of the body's fluids

sentences.source_text: The data is transmitted to a receiver, and patients can see a constant reading of their blood sugar levels.

triples.o: a constant reading of their blood sugar levels

sentences.source_text: I have participated in clinical trials for three different CGM devices, and I found them all to be quite accurate.

triples.o: clinical trials for three different CGM devices
them all to be quite accurate

sentences.source_text: Some sensors transmit data directly to an insulin pump, so you can use the reading to adjust and fine-tune the amount of insulin being pumped into the body.

triples.o: the reading to adjust and fine-tune the amount of insulin being pumped into the body

sentences.source_text: The patient still has to test his or her blood sugar to confirm the sensor is properly calibrated, but it's amazing to be able to see constant readings and graphs of what your blood sugar is doing and better manage blood sugars.

triples.o: amazing to be able to see constant readings and graphs of what your blood sugar is doing and better manage blood sugars

sentences.source_text: CGM technology is a step toward the ultimate goal of controlling type I diabetes using what's called a "closed loop system."

triples.o: a step toward the ultimate goal of controlling type I diabetes using what's called a "closed loop system"

sentences.source_text: This means diabetics someday will be able to wear a pump that gathers data from a sensor so precise that the pump can be programmed to act on its own and make adjustments all by itself.

triples.o: a pump that gathers data from a sensor
to act on its own and make adjustments all by itself

sentences.source_text: The current systems are "open looped," meaning the diabetic controls when the doses are given.
The goal is to eventually create a pump that is more like a robotic delivery system, and all the patient has to do is wear it.

triples.o: to eventually create a pump that is more like a robotic delivery system

sentences.source_text: Once that technology is perfected, this type of device would be implanted, which would be the next best thing to not having diabetes.

triples.o: the next best thing to not having diabetes

sentences.source_text: I mentioned to my sister Robin that I had tracked down Dr. Deputy for this article.

triples.o: my sister Robin that I had tracked down Dr. Deputy for this article

sentences.source_text: She told me something I didn't know when I was 7.

triples.o: me something I didn't know when I was 7

sentences.source_text: Forty years ago, before the advent of all of the cool diabetes technology such as insulin pumps, continuous glucose monitors and blood sugar testing, parents were given a fairly grim prediction for how their child would fare.

triples.o: a fairly grim prediction for how their child would fare

sentences.source_text: Dr. Deputy told my parents that I would probably only live to the age of 20 or 30, which apparently -- but not surprisingly -- had the whole family freaked out.

triples.o: my parents that I would probably only live to the age of 20 or 30

sentences.source_text: I spoke with Dr. Deputy, who recently retired from his pediatric practice, and he seemed to remember me.

When I asked him about his dismal prognosis for my survival, he said sadly, "At that time, that was what we told parents back then. ... Everything changes with time -- diabetes, leukemia -- technology has changed with the pump and the equipment."

triples.o: him about his dismal prognosis for my survival
At that time, that was what we told parents back then. ... Everything changes with time -- diabetes, leukemia -- technology has changed with the pump and the equipment.
with the pump and the equipment

sentences.source_text: I think he was relieved that his prognosis was wrong.

triples.o: he was relieved that his prognosis was wrong

sentences.source_text: I know that I certainly am.
In fact, I am hoping to live another 40 years!
E-mail to a friend .

sentences.source_text: = Hurricane Nicole ( 1998 ) =
Hurricane Nicole was the last hurricane in the 1998 Atlantic hurricane season.

sentences.source_text: It developed from a frontal low to the south of the Azores on November 24 , and quickly strengthened to reach winds of 70 mph ( 110 km / h ) as it moved to the west - southwest.

sentences.source_text: An approaching upper - level trough moved over the system , producing strong wind shear which rapidly weakened Nicole to a tropical depression.

sentences.source_text: After the trough passed , the system entered an area of lighter shear , and steadily restrengthened.
An approaching cold front turned it to the northeast , and while moving over anomalously warm sea surface temperatures Nicole attained hurricane status.
It reached a peak intensity of 85 mph ( 135 km / h ) on December 1 , and rapidly weakened over the waters of the north Atlantic Ocean.

triples.o: a peak intensity of 85 mph ( 135 km / h ) on December 1
the waters of the north Atlantic Ocean

sentences.source_text: Nicole is one of five December Atlantic hurricanes on record.

A strong frontal low located several hundred miles south of the Azores

sentences.source_text: After it began moving steadily to the west , a tightly - wrapped band of convection developed near the center , and it is estimated the system developed into Tropical Depression Fourteen on November 24 while located 725 miles ( 1 , 165 km ) south of Lajes , Azores.

sentences.source_text: The system quickly intensified as it moved west - southwestward , and strengthened into Tropical Storm Nicole six hours after forming.

sentences.source_text: Tropical Storm Nicole was a small storm , and upon first forming tropical storm force winds extended only 60 miles ( 95 km ) from the center.

triples.o: the system was a tropical storm

sentences.source_text: The storm moved through an area of marginal sea surface temperatures of 77 ° F ( 25 ° C ).

sentences.source_text: An intermittent eye feature of the small area of deep convection , and Nicole quickly strengthened to reach an initial peak intensity of 70 mph ( 115 km / h ).

sentences.source_text: After maintaining its peak for around twelve hours , an upper - level trough moved rapidly eastward over Nicole , which produced high levels of wind shear.
The shear weakened the storm , and by late on November 25 the center became exposed to the west of the diminishing deep convection.

Operationally , advisories were discontinued , and regeneration was considered unlikely.

triples.o: advisories for Nicole on November 27 while located about 1 , 515 miles ( 2 , 440 km ) west - southwest of the Canary Islands

sentences.source_text: = WASP - 44 =
WASP - 44 is a G - type star in constellation Cetus without any planets in orbit.

sentences.source_text: The star is slightly less massive and slightly smaller than the Sun ; it is also slightly cooler , but is more metal - rich.

The star was observed by SuperWASP , an organization in search of planets , starting in 2009 ; manual follow - up observations used WASP - 44 's spectrum and measurements of its radial velocity led to the discovery of the transiting planet WASP - 44b.

The planet and its star were presented along with WASP - 45b and WASP - 46b on May 17 , 2011 by a team of scientists testing the idea that Hot Jupiters tend to have circular orbits , an assumption that is made when the orbital eccentricity of such planets are not well - constrained.

the orbital eccentricity of such planets are not well - constrained

sentences.source_text: = = Observational history = =

sentences.source_text: Observations of the star revealed a periodic decrease in its brightness.

sentences.source_text: WASP - South , along with the SuperWASP - North station at the Roque de los Muchachos Observatory on the Canary Islands , collected 15 @,@ 755 photometric observations , allowing scientists to produce a more accurate light curve.

triples.o: scientists to produce a more accurate light curve

sentences.source_text: Another set of observations yielded a 6 @,@ 000 point photometric data set , but the light curve was prepared late and was not considered in the discovery paper.

sentences.source_text: In 2010 , a European science team investigated the star using the CORALIE spectrograph and collected seventeen spectra of WASP - 44.
From the spectra , radial velocity measurements were extrapolated.

Analysis of collected CORALIE data ruled out the possibility that the detected radial velocity was caused by the blended spectrum of a spectroscopic binary star , supporting the possibility that the body orbiting WASP - 44 was indeed a planet , designated WASP - 44b.

triples.o: the possibility that the detected radial velocity was caused by the blended spectrum of a spectroscopic binary star
the possibility that the body orbiting WASP - 44 was indeed a planet

sentences.source_text: The Leonhard Euler Telescope at La Silla Observatory in Chile was used to follow up on the discovery circling WASP - 44 , searching for a point at which the planet transited , or crossed in front of , its host star.

triples.s: The Leonhard Euler Telescope at La Silla Observatory in Chile

sentences.source_text: One transit was detected.

WASP - 44 , its recently discovered planet , the planets orbiting WASP - 45 and WASP - 46 , and a discussion exploring the validity of the common assumption amongst scientists that closely orbiting Hot Jupiter planets have highly circular orbits unless proven otherwise , were reported in a single discovery paper that was published on May 17 , 2011 by the Royal Astronomical Society.

sentences.source_text: = = Characteristics = =
WASP - 44 is a G - type star ( the same class of star as the Sun ) that is located in the Cetus constellation.
WASP - 44 has a mass that is 0. 951 times that of the Sun.
In terms of size , WASP - 44 has a radius that is 0. 927 times that of the Sun.
WASP - 44 has an effective temperature of 5410 K , cooler than the Sun.
However , the star is metal - rich with relation to the Sun.
Its measured metallicity is [ Fe / H ]
= 0. 06 , or 1. 148 times that the amount of iron found in the Sun.

triples.o: 1. 148 times that the amount of iron found in the Sun

sentences.source_text: WASP - 44 's chromosphere ( outermost layer ) is not active.
The star also does not rotate at a high velocity.
The star has an apparent magnitude of 12. 9.
It cannot be seen from Earth with the naked eye.

triples.p: cannot be seen from Earth with

sentences.source_text: = Planetary system =
= There is one known planet in the orbit of WASP - 44 : WASP - 44b.
The planet is a Hot Jupiter with a mass of 0. 889 Jupiters.
Its radius is 1. 14 times that of Jupiter.

sentences.source_text: = French cruiser Amiral Charner =
Amiral Charner was an armored cruiser built for the French Navy in the 1890s , the name ship of her class.
She spent most of her career in the Mediterranean , although she was sent to China during the Boxer Rebellion of 1900 – 01.
The ship was assigned to the International Squadron off the island of Crete during the Greco - Turkish War of 1897 to protect French interests and citizens.

triples.o: the International Squadron off the island of Crete

sentences.source_text: Amiral Charner spent most of the first decade of the 20th century as a training ship or in reserve.

triples.o: the first decade of the 20th century

sentences.source_text: The ship was recommissioned when World War I began in 1914 and escorted convoys for several months before she was assigned to the Eastern Mediterranean to blockade the Ottoman - controlled coast.
During this time , she helped to rescue several thousand Armenians from Syria during the Armenian Genocide of 1915.
Amiral Charner was sunk in early 1916 by a German submarine , with only a single survivor rescued.
= = Design and description = =
The Amiral Charner - class ships were designed to be smaller and cheaper than the preceding armored cruiser design , the Dupuy de Lôme.

triples.p: were designed to be smaller and cheaper than

sentences.source_text: Like the older ship , they were intended to fill the commerce - raiding strategy of the Jeune École.

triples.o: the commerce - raiding strategy of the Jeune École

sentences.source_text: The ship measured 106. 12 meters ( 348 ft 2 in ) between perpendiculars , with a beam of 14. 04 meters ( 46 ft 1 in ).
Amiral Charner had a forward draft of 5. 55 meters ( 18 ft 3 in ) and drew 6. 06 meters ( 19 ft 11 in ) aft.
She displaced 4 , 748 metric tons ( 4 , 673 long tons ) at normal load and 4 , 990 metric tons ( 4 , 910 long tons ) at deep load.
The Amiral Charner class had two triple - expansion steam engines , each driving a single propeller shaft.
Steam for the engines was provided by 16 Belleville boilers and they were rated at a total of 8 , 300 metric horsepower ( 6 , 100 kW ) using forced draught.

Amiral Charner had a designed speed of 19 knots ( 35 km / h ; 22 mph ) , but during sea trials on 18 July 1895 the engines produced 8 , 956 metric horsepower ( 6 , 587 kW ) , but only gave a maximum speed of 18. 4 knots ( 34. 1 km / h ; 21. 2 mph ).

sentences.source_text: = 55 Cancri b =

55 Cancri b ( abbreviated 55 Cnc b ) , occasionally designated 55 Cancri Ab ( to distinguish it from the star 55 Cancri B ) , also named Galileo , is an extrasolar planet orbiting the Sun - like star 55 Cancri A every 14. 65 days.
It is the second planet in order of distance from its star , and is an example of a hot Jupiter , or possibly rather " warm Jupiter ".

sentences.source_text: In July 2014 the International Astronomical Union launched a process for giving proper names to certain exoplanets and their host stars.

sentences.source_text: The winning name was submitted by the Royal Netherlands Association for Meteorology and Astronomy of the Netherlands.

sentences.source_text: It honors early - 17th century astronomer and physicist Galileo Galilei.

sentences.source_text: By making sensitive measurements of the Doppler shift of the spectrum of 55 Cancri A , a 15 - day periodicity was detected.

sentences.source_text: = French cruiser Chanzy =
Chanzy was an Amiral Charner - class armored cruiser built for the French Navy in the 1890s.

Upon completion , she served in the Mediterranean Squadron and she was assigned to the International Squadron off the island of Crete during the Greco - Turkish War of 1897 to protect French interests and citizens.

triples.o: the International Squadron off the island of Crete

sentences.source_text: The ship was in reserve for several years in the middle of the first decade of the 20th century before she was transferred to French Indochina in 1906.

triples.o: the middle of the first decade of the 20th century

sentences.source_text: Chanzy ran aground off the Chinese coast in mid - 1907 , where she proved impossible to refloat and was destroyed in place after her crew was rescued without loss.

triples.o: her crew was rescued without loss

sentences.source_text: = = Design and description = =
The Amiral Charner - class ships were designed to be smaller and cheaper than the preceding armored cruiser design , the Dupuy de Lôme.

triples.o: the preceding armored cruiser design, the Dupuy de Lôme

sentences.source_text: Like the older ship , they were intended to fill the commerce - raiding strategy of the Jeune École.

triples.o: the commerce-raiding strategy of the Jeune École

sentences.source_text: Chanzy measured 106. 12 meters ( 348 ft 2 in ) between perpendiculars , with a beam of 14. 04 meters ( 46 ft 1 in ).
The ship had a forward draft of 5. 55 meters ( 18 ft 3 in ) and drew 6. 06 meters ( 19 ft 11 in ) aft.
She displaced 4 , 748 metric tons ( 4 , 673 long tons ) at normal load and 4 , 990 metric tons ( 4 , 910 long tons ) at deep load.
The Amiral Charner class had two triple - expansion steam engines , each driving a single propeller shaft.
Steam for the engines was provided by 16 Belleville boilers and they were rated at a total of 8 , 300 metric horsepower ( 6 , 100 kW ) using forced draft.

Amiral Charner had a designed speed of 19 knots ( 35 km / h ; 22 mph ) and carried up to 535 metric tons ( 527 long tons ; 590 short tons ) of coal that allowed her to steam for 4 , 000 nautical miles ( 7 , 400 km ; 4 , 600 mi ) at a speed of 10 knots ( 19 km / h ; 12 mph ).
The ships of the Amiral Charner class had a main armament that consisted of two Canon de 194 mm Modèle 1887 guns that were mounted in single gun turrets , one each fore and aft of the superstructure.

triples.s: The ships of the Amiral Charner class

sentences.source_text: The Manuscript itself covers the years 1912 through 1932 in which the Oligarchy (or "Iron Heel") arose in the United States.
In Asia, Japan conquered East Asia and created its own empire, India gained independence, and Europe became socialist.

sentences.source_text: In North America, the Oligarchy maintains power for three centuries until the Revolution succeeds and ushers in the Brotherhood of Man.
During the years of the novel, the First Revolt is described and preparations for the Second Revolt are discussed.

From the perspective of Everhard, the imminent Second Revolt is sure to succeed but, from the distant future perspective of Meredith, we readers realize that Everhard's hopes were to be crushed for centuries to come.

sentences.source_text: Editor's note: In our Behind the Scenes series, CNN correspondents share their experiences in covering news and analyze the stories behind the events.
Here, Soledad O'Brien takes users inside a jail where many of the inmates are mentally ill.
An inmate housed on the "forgotten floor," where many mentally ill inmates are housed in Miami before trial.

triples.s: An inmate housed on the "forgotten floor"

sentences.source_text: MIAMI, Florida (CNN) -- The ninth floor of the Miami-Dade pretrial detention facility is dubbed the "forgotten floor."

triples.s: The ninth floor of the Miami-Dade pretrial detention facility

sentences.source_text: Here, inmates with the most severe mental illnesses are incarcerated until they're ready to appear in court.

triples.s: inmates with the most severe mental illnesses
triples.o: they're ready to appear in court

sentences.source_text: Most often, they face drug charges or charges of assaulting an officer --charges that Judge Steven Leifman says are usually "avoidable felonies."

triples.o: drug charges or charges of assaulting an officer

sentences.source_text: He says the arrests often result from confrontations with police.

triples.o: the arrests often result from confrontations with police

sentences.source_text: Mentally ill people often won't do what they're told when police arrive on the scene -- confrontation seems to exacerbate their illness and they become more paranoid, delusional, and less likely to follow directions, according to Leifman.

triples.o: more paranoid, delusional, and less likely to follow directions

sentences.source_text: So, they end up on the ninth floor severely mentally disturbed, but not getting any real help because they're in jail.
We toured the jail with Leifman.
He is well known in Miami as an advocate for justice and the mentally ill.

triples.o: Miami as an advocate for justice and the mentally ill

sentences.source_text: Even though we were not exactly welcomed with open arms by the guards, we were given permission to shoot videotape and tour the floor.

triples.p: were not exactly welcomed with open arms by

triples.o: shoot videotape and tour the floor

sentences.source_text: Go inside the 'forgotten floor' » .
At first, it's hard to determine where the people are.
The prisoners are wearing sleeveless robes.
Imagine cutting holes for arms and feet in a heavy wool sleeping bag -- that's kind of what they look like.

triples.o: a heavy wool sleeping bag with holes for arms and feet

sentences.source_text: They're designed to keep the mentally ill patients from injuring themselves.

triples.o: the mentally ill patients from injuring themselves

sentences.source_text: That's also why they have no shoes, laces or mattresses.
Leifman says about one-third of all people in Miami-Dade county jails are mentally ill.

triples.o: about one-third of all people in Miami-Dade county jails are mentally ill

sentences.source_text: So, he says, the sheer volume is overwhelming the system, and the result is what we see on the ninth floor.

triples.o: what we see on the ninth floor

sentences.source_text: Of course, it is a jail, so it's not supposed to be warm and comforting, but the lights glare, the cells are tiny and it's loud.
We see two, sometimes three men -- sometimes in the robes, sometimes naked, lying or sitting in their cells.

triples.o: sometimes in the robes, sometimes naked

sentences.source_text: "I am the son of the president. You need to get me out of here!" one man shouts at me.
He is absolutely serious, convinced that help is on the way -- if only he could reach the White House.
Leifman tells me that these prisoner-patients will often circulate through the system, occasionally stabilizing in a mental hospital, only to return to jail to face their charges.

triples.o: these prisoner-patients will often circulate through the system

sentences.source_text: It's brutally unjust, in his mind, and he has become a strong advocate for changing things in Miami.

triples.o: a strong advocate for changing things in Miami

sentences.source_text: Over a meal later, we talk about how things got this way for mental patients.

triples.o: how things got this way for mental patients

sentences.source_text: Leifman says 200 years ago people were considered "lunatics" and they were locked up in jails even if they had no charges against them.
They were just considered unfit to be in society.
Over the years, he says, there was some public outcry, and the mentally ill were moved out of jails and into hospitals.
But Leifman says many of these mental hospitals were so horrible they were shut down.

triples.o: many of these mental hospitals were so horrible

sentences.source_text: Where did the patients go?
Nowhere.
The streets.
They became, in many cases, the homeless, he says.

triples.o: they became, in many cases, the homeless

sentences.source_text: They never got treatment.
Leifman says in 1955 there were more than half a million people in state mental hospitals, and today that number has been reduced 90 percent, and 40,000 to 50,000 people are in mental hospitals.

triples.o: in 1955 there were more than half a million people in state mental hospitals

sentences.source_text: The judge says he's working to change this.

Starting in 2008, many inmates who would otherwise have been brought to the "forgotten floor"  will instead be sent to a new mental health facility -- the first step on a journey toward long-term treatment, not just punishment.

triples.p: would otherwise have been brought to

triples.o: the first step on a journey toward long-term treatment, not just punishment

sentences.source_text: Leifman says it's not the complete answer, but it's a start.
Leifman says the best part is that it's a win-win solution.

triples.o: the best part is that it's a win-win solution

sentences.source_text: The patients win, the families are relieved, and the state saves money by simply not cycling these prisoners through again and again.

triples.o: simply not cycling these prisoners through again and again

sentences.source_text: And, for Leifman, justice is served.

sentences.source_text: = Gyles v Wilcox =
Gyles v Wilcox ( 1740 ) 26 ER 489 was a decision of the Court of Chancery of England that established the doctrine of fair abridgement , which would later evolve into the concept of fair use.

triples.o: the Court of Chancery of England

sentences.source_text: The case was heard and the opinion written by Philip Yorke , 1st Earl of Hardwicke , and concerned Fletcher Gyles , a bookseller who had published a copy of Matthew Hale 's Pleas of the Crown.

triples.o: a copy of Matthew Hale's Pleas of the Crown

sentences.source_text: Soon after the initial publication , the publishers Wilcox and Nutt hired a writer named Barrow to abridge the book , and repackaged it as Modern Crown Law.
Gyles sued for a stay on the book 's publishing , claiming his rights under the Statute of Anne had been infringed.

triples.o: a stay on the book's publishing
his rights under the Statute of Anne had been infringed

sentences.source_text: The main issues in the case were whether or not abridgements of a work inherently constituted copyright infringement , or whether they could qualify as a separate , new work.

triples.s: The main issues in the case
triples.o: whether abridgements of a work inherently constituted copyright infringement

triples.s: The main issues in the case
triples.o: whether abridgements could qualify as a separate, new work

sentences.source_text: Lord Hartwicke ruled that abridgements fell under two categories : " true abridgements " and " coloured shortenings ".
True abridgements presented a true effort on the part of the editor , and by this effort , constituted a new work which did not infringe upon the copyright of the original.

triples.o: a true effort on the part of the editor

sentences.source_text: Leaving it to literary and legal experts to decide , Hartwicke ruled that Modern Crown Law was not a true abridgement , but merely a duplication intending to circumvent the law.

triples.p: ruled that Modern Crown Law was not

triples.o: a duplication intending to circumvent the law

sentences.source_text: The case set a legal precedent which has shaped copyright law to the present day.

triples.o: copyright law to the present day

sentences.source_text: It established the common law doctrine of fair abridgement , which was cited in other cases , ultimately building up to the idea of fair use.

triples.o: the common law doctrine of fair abridgement

triples.s: the common law doctrine of fair abridgement
the common law doctrine of fair abridgement

sentences.source_text: The opinion also recognised the author 's right to a work through the nature of the labour it took to produce it , shifting copyright away from publishing rights and towards the idea of serving the greater good by encouraging the production of new , useful works.

triples.o: the author's right to a work through the nature of the labour it took to produce it
the idea of serving the greater good by encouraging the production of new, useful works

sentences.source_text: = = Facts = =
Fletcher Gyles , an English bookseller , had previously published a book entitled Matthew Hale 's Pleas of the Crown , for which he had purchased the exclusive publishing rights.

triples.o: a book entitled Matthew Hale's Pleas of the Crown

sentences.source_text: Around the same time , publishers Wilcox and Nutt paid a writer named Barrow to abridge the book , circulating it under the title Modern Crown Law.

triples.o: it under the title Modern Crown Law

sentences.source_text: Gyles alleged that Modern Crown Law was a near verbatim copy of his publication , with only minor alterations , including the translation of Latin and French passages into English and cutting old , obsolete laws.

triples.p: alleged that Modern Crown Law was
triples.o: a near verbatim copy of his publication
including the translation of Latin and French passages into English and cutting old, obsolete laws

sentences.source_text: Seeking to protect his printing rights , Gyles sued both Wilcox and Nutt , along with Barrow , for a stay on the publication.

triples.o: Wilcox and Nutt, along with Barrow

sentences.source_text: = = Arguments = =

The case involved whether Wilcox , Barrow , and Nutt had violated Gyles ' publishing rights as defined under the Statute of Anne , particularly the section stating that an author , or purchaser of an author 's copyrights as Gyles was , " shall have the sole Liberty of Printing and Reprinting such Book and Books for the Term of four - teen years. "

triples.o: Wilcox, Barrow, and Nutt had violated Gyles' publishing rights
an author, or purchaser of an author's copyrights as Gyles was, "shall have the sole Liberty of Printing and Reprinting such Book and Books for the Term of four-teen years"

sentences.source_text: Philip Yorke , 1st Earl of Hardwicke presided over and decided the case.
Browning , Gyles ' attorney , cited a case which had also appeared before Hardwicke , that of Read v Hodges.
In that case , a publisher attempted to circumvent the rights of the author of Czar Peter the Great by including all three volumes in one and cutting several pages.

triples.o: the rights of the author of Czar Peter the Great

sentences.source_text: Hardwicke rejected the argument , however , declaring that the former case had been decided merely on a motion , and that he had given his decision and statements without the thought he would have given a normal hearing.

triples.o: his decision and statements without the thought he would have given a normal hearing

sentences.source_text: Hardwicke further took contention with the Attorney General for England and Wales ' assertion that the Statute of Anne provided a publishing monopoly , instead interpreting the act as one meant to promote public education and the public good.

triples.o: the Attorney General for England and Wales' assertion

triples.s: the Attorney General for England and Wales' assertion
triples.o: the Statute of Anne provided a publishing monopoly
the act as one meant to promote public education and the public good

sentences.source_text: As Hardwicke had decided to interpret the Statute of Anne as for the public good , the main question of the case became which " any such book or books " the act referred to and protected.

triples.o: the Statute of Anne as for the public good

triples.s: the main question of the case
triples.o: which "any such book or books" the act referred to and protected

sentences.source_text: The defendants argued that his abridgement must be considered separate from the original work published by Gyles.

triples.o: his abridgement must be considered separate from the original work published by Gyles
the original work published by Gyles

sentences.source_text: The defendant 's lawyers furthered pushed the court to try the case as if the abridgement had been recorded in the Stationers ' Register , an action that would have given Wilcox and Nutt the right to publish their book , and the lawsuit brought against a second , unique book.

triples.o: the court to try the case as if the abridgement had been recorded in the Stationers' Register
Wilcox and Nutt the right to publish their book
the lawsuit brought against a second, unique book

sentences.source_text: Therefore , the only question before the court was whether the second book differentiated sufficiently from the first.

triples.s: the only question before the court
triples.o: whether the second book differentiated sufficiently from the first

sentences.source_text: Further , the attorneys for the defendants argued that the book was not a direct transcription , but that several chapters had been omitted , while other , original sections had been added to the Wilcox and Nutt publication.

triples.p: argued that the book was not
argued that several chapters had been omitted

sentences.source_text: They further pointed to the fact that the Gyles ' publication consisted of 275 sheets , whereas the abridgement contained only 35 sheets.

triples.o: the fact that the Gyles' publication consisted of 275 sheets, whereas the abridgement contained only 35 sheets

sentences.source_text: = = Judgment = =

The opinion , written by Hardwicke , found that a true abridgement of a published book may be considered an entirely separate , new work , as the abridgement showed the labour , originality , education , and judgement of the editor.

triples.s: a true abridgement of a published book

triples.o: the labour, originality, education, and judgement of the editor

sentences.source_text: This new book did not run the risk of infringing the rights of the author or bookseller who owned the publishing rights.

triples.p: did not run the risk of infringing
triples.o: the rights of the author or bookseller who owned the publishing rights

sentences.source_text: However , Lord Hardwicke drew a distinction between works " fairly made " and those " colourably shortened ".

Hardwicke refused to compare the books himself to determine whether Modern Crown Law was indeed a fair abridgement , or to force a judge and jury to sit and hear both books read , instead opting to have two legal experts and a literary master read the books and report the findings to the court.

triples.o: a judge and jury to sit and hear both books read
two legal experts and a literary master read the books and report the findings to the court

sentences.source_text: The parties were allowed to choose these examiners , in a way leaving the case to arbitration.

After a week in which the parties were given a chance to make amends outside of court , the book in question was ruled a colourable shortening , created only to circumvent the law , and thus was an infringement of Gyles ' printing rights.

triples.o: an infringement of Gyles' printing rights

sentences.source_text: In his decision , Hartwicke went counter to the prevailing view that the Statute of Anne should be interpreted very strictly , proclaiming , " I am quite of a different opinion , and that it ought to receive a liberal construction , for it is far from being a monopoly , as it is intended to secure the property of books in the authors themselves , or the purchasers of the copy , as some recompense for their pains and labour in such works as may be of use to the learned world. "

triples.o: the prevailing view that the Statute of Anne should be interpreted very strictly
"I am quite of a different opinion"
the property of books in the authors themselves, or the purchasers of the copy

sentences.source_text: = = Consequences = =

The case established the doctrine of fair abridgement , which allowed that abridgements displaying a fair amount of labour on the part of the editor , and that differed from the original published work in a significant way , could not be copyright violations.

triples.o: abridgements displaying a fair amount of labour on the part of the editor could not be copyright violations
abridgements that differed from the original published work in a significant way could not be copyright violations

sentences.source_text: This in effect raised the abridger to the level of an author.

triples.o: the abridger to the level of an author

sentences.source_text: The decision did not define the exact parameters that would qualify a work as a valid abridgement.

triples.o: the exact parameters that would qualify a work as a valid abridgement

sentences.source_text: This distinction came with a later case involving an abridgement of Hawksworth 's Voyages , in a decision written by Lord Chancellor Apsley.

triples.o: a later case involving an abridgement of Hawksworth's Voyages

sentences.source_text: This concept of fair abridgement eventually evolved through common law , initiated from Gyles v Wilcox , into the current concept of fair use.

triples.o: the current concept of fair use

sentences.source_text: Hardwicke 's decision also added the exercise of personal judgement to the list of admissible defences against the charge of copyright infringement , adding to the growing case law establishing that British copyright would be based on labour and not on originality.

triples.o: the exercise of personal judgement to the list of admissible defences against the charge of copyright infringement
the growing case law establishing that British copyright would be based on labour and not on originality

sentences.source_text: The opinion advanced the position that copyright law should serve the public interest by promoting the creation of new educational and useful works , rather than focusing on publishing rights.

triples.o: the position that copyright law should serve the public interest by promoting the creation of new educational and useful works

sentences.source_text: The case played a significant role in the development of English copyright law.

triples.o: the development of English copyright law

sentences.source_text: The United States federal courts have cited the case as recently as the 1980s.

triples.o: the case as recently as the 1980s

sentences.source_text: = Æthelbert of York =
Æthelbert ( sometimes Æthelberht , Albert , Ælberht , Aethelberht , or Ælbert ; died 8 November 780 ) was an eighth century scholar , teacher , and Archbishop of York.
Related to his predecessor at York , he became a monk at an early age and was in charge of the cathedral 's library and school before becoming archbishop.

triples.o: a monk at an early age

sentences.source_text: He taught a number of missionaries and scholars , including Alcuin , at the school.

triples.o: a number of missionaries and scholars

sentences.source_text: While archbishop Æthelbert rebuilt the cathedral and sent missionaries to the Continent.
Æthelbert retired before his death , and during his retirement built another church in York.
= = Early life = =

Æthelbert , was the teacher and intimate friend of Alcuin , whose poem on the saints and prelates of the Church of York , Versus de Patribus Regibus et de Sanctis et Pontificibus Ecclesiæ Eboracensis , is the principal source of information concerning Æthelbert 's life.

triples.s: Versus de Patribus Regibus et de Sanctis et Pontificibus Ecclesiæ Eboracensis
triples.o: the principal source of information concerning Æthelbert's life

sentences.source_text: He was a kinsman of his predecessor Ecgbert , who was brother to Eadberht , King of Northumbria.

triples.o: Æthelbert in a monastery as a young child

sentences.source_text: Ecgbert ordained Æthelbert as a priest put him in charge of the school.

triples.o: Æthelbert in charge of the school

sentences.source_text: Æthelbert was instrumental in forming a library at York , which was probably the largest contemporary collection of books to be found in Europe outside of Rome.

triples.o: the largest contemporary collection of books in Europe outside of Rome

triples.o: several Latin and Greek classical authors

sentences.source_text: Æthelbert , in his search for books , travelled far , and we know that he visited Rome among other places.

sentences.source_text: = = Archbishop = =

triples.o: his pallium from Pope Adrian I in 773

sentences.source_text: Alcuin was appointed head of the cathedral school after Æthelbert became archbishop.
Much of Alcuin 's description of Æthelbert 's time as archbishop has the flavour of a panegyric , as Alcuin praised Æthelbert as a model bishop suitable for other bishops to use as a role model.

triples.s: Alcuin's description of Æthelbert's time as archbishop

triples.o: Æthelbert as a model bishop suitable for other bishops to use as a role model

sentences.source_text: Æthelbert rebuilt York Minster , which had been destroyed by fire in 741 , giving Eanbald and Alcuin the job of overseeing the construction.

triples.o: Eanbald and Alcuin the job of overseeing the construction

sentences.source_text: Alcuin wrote that it had bright windows and ceilings and that the liturgical vessels and altars were decorated with precious metals and gems.
The new building also had 30 altars as well as upper apartments.
Æthelbert dedicated one of the altars to Saint Paul and it was located on the location where Edwin , the first Christian king of Northumbria , was baptized.

triples.o: one of the altars to Saint Paul
the location where Edwin was baptized
the first Christian king of Northumbria

sentences.source_text: Æthelbert sent out missionaries to the pagans of Northern Europe , among them Alubert and Liudger , who went to northern Germany.

triples.o: missionaries to the pagans of Northern Europe

sentences.source_text: Liudger had earlier been a pupil at the school in York , and went on to become the first Bishop of Munster.

triples.o: a pupil at the school in York

sentences.source_text: Æthelbert was the recipient of letters from one of the missionaries - Lull , the Archbishop of Mainz , assuming that Lull 's correspondent " Coena " , who is an archbishop and who was being asked for the works of Bede , is actually Æthelbert , as most historians seem convinced of.
Books were sent to the missionaries from the York library.

triples.o: the missionaries from the York library

sentences.source_text: In 774 , Æthelbert called a council which deposed Alhred the King of Northumbria and sent the ex - king north into exile with the Picts.

triples.o: the ex-king north into exile with the Picts

sentences.source_text: The cause of the deposition may have been related to missionary work.
The historian D. P. Kirby feels that Æthelbert was not a supporter of Alhred prior to his deposition.

triples.o: Æthelbert was not a supporter of Alhred prior to his deposition

sentences.source_text: Alhred was replaced with Æthelred , who was replaced in 778 by Ælfwald , the son of Oswulf.
Kirby sees Æthelbert 's withdrawal of support as instrumental in the deposition of Æthelred , noting that Ælfwald was closely related to Æthelbert , unlike both Alhred and Æthelred.

triples.o: Æthelbert's withdrawal of support as instrumental in the deposition of Æthelred

sentences.source_text: Kirby also notes that medieval chroniclers noted that Æthelbert is said to have not " spared evil kings ".

triples.o: that medieval chroniclers noted that Æthelbert is said to have not " spared evil kings "

sentences.source_text: = = Retirement and death = =
Æthelbert retired some time before his death , consecrating Eanbald as his successor.
The exact date this occurred is unclear.
Alcuin gives a date corresponding to July 778 , but it could be 777 too.
Eanbald 's position may have just been as an associate bishop , with Æthelbert remaining in office until his death while sharing the office with Eanbald.
During his retirement , he had constructed a new church dedicated to Alma Sophia.

triples.o: a new church dedicated to Alma Sophia

sentences.source_text: He lived long enough to consecrate the new church , ten days before his death on 8 November.
